summary.name: Large-Cap Value Momentum Long-Short
summary.author: Derek Melchin
summary.description: The strategy runs a monthly cross sectional long short equity portfolio in large liquid US common stocks, seeking to capture the combined value and intermediate term momentum premia. summary.description_full:
The strategy runs a monthly cross sectional long short equity portfolio in large liquid US common stocks, seeking to capture the combined value and intermediate term momentum premia. The investable universe is rebuilt at the start of each month from the 500 highest dollar volume US stocks with fundamental data and price above 1, then restricted to common shares only (excluding ADRs, REITs, and the Financials sector) and narrowed to the 50 largest by market capitalization. Signals are value (book to market proxied by inverse price to book) and momentum (roughly 12 month total return excluding the most recent month), designed to avoid short term reversal while targeting persistent return continuation.

At each monthly rebalance using daily data and fundamentals, securities are ranked on value and momentum, transformed into demeaned cross sectional scores, and averaged to form a composite factor. Portfolio weights are proportional to this composite and normalized so the sum of absolute weights equals 1, producing an approximately dollar neutral long short book with 100 percent gross exposure and minimal net market exposure. Positions are reset to target weights at each rebalance; securities leaving the universe are liquidated immediately. The alpha source is factor dispersion within mega and large cap equities rather than directional market timing.

raw.name: Large-Cap Value Momentum Long-Short

author.name: Derek Melchin
author.bio:
Quantitative Developer at QuantConnect. Studied Computer Science and Finance at the University of Lethbridge. Competitor in the 2020-1 Rotman International Trading Competitions. 
See my latest posts at derekmelchin.com.
